_meta.description: MedGuard-AI Medical History Contraindications. FOR RESEARCH USE ONLY.

asthma.reason: Non-selective beta-blockers block beta-2 receptors in bronchial smooth muscle, causing bronchoconstriction and potentially fatal bronchospasm. Selective beta-1 blockers (e.g. Bisoprolol, Metoprolol) require extreme caution but are not strictly contraindicated if needed for cardiovascular disease.
safe_alternatives: Selective beta-1 blockers (Bisoprolol) with EXTREME caution only if no alternative

renal failure.reason: NSAIDs reduce renal perfusion. Metformin accumulates causing lactic acidosis. Nitrofurantoin ineffective and toxic in severe renal impairment.
safe_alternatives: Insulin for diabetes in severe CKD

liver failure.reason: Impaired hepatic metabolism leads to drug accumulation and worsening hepatic injury.

hypertension.reason: Sympathomimetics raise blood pressure through alpha-adrenergic vasoconstriction. NSAIDs blunt antihypertensive effect.

type 2 diabetes.reason: Corticosteroids cause steroid-induced hyperglycaemia. Thiazides impair insulin secretion. Atypical antipsychotics cause insulin resistance.
safe_alternatives: Use lowest effective corticosteroid dose. Monitor blood glucose during steroid courses.

pregnancy.reason: These agents are confirmed teratogens or cause significant fetal harm. Tetracyclines stain teeth, NSAIDs cause premature ductus closure, ACE inhibitors cause renal defects.

heart failure.reason: NSAIDs cause fluid retention. CCBs (non-dihydropyridine) depress myocardial contractility. Thiazolidinediones cause fluid retention via renal mechanism.
safe_alternatives: ACE inhibitor + beta-blocker as standard HF therapy

peptic ulcer disease.reason: NSAIDs inhibit COX-1 prostaglandin synthesis, disrupting gastroprotective mucus. Steroids impair mucosal healing.

hyperlipidemia.reason: These agents worsen the lipid profile, counteracting treatment goals.

epilepsy.reason: These agents lower the seizure threshold, risking breakthrough seizures in controlled epilepsy.
safe_alternatives: Alternative antipsychotics with lower seizure risk
